text.title: backup-database-postgres Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for backup-database-postgres. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every pg_dump script MUST verify the dump file with `pg_restore -l "$BACKUP_PATH" > /dev/null` before uploading to remote storage. A backup that fails pg_restore -l is corrupt and must trigger an alert — not a silent upload.
rule.rationale: pg_dump can produce a partial file when the source crashes mid-export or when disk fills. Silent uploads create the illusion of a backup without the substance. The verification adds under one second for most database sizes.

rule.statement: Repositories using WAL archiving for PITR MUST monitor `pg_stat_archiver.failed_count` and alert when it increases. Silently failed WAL segments create invisible gaps that make the PITR chain unrestorable from the point of the first failure forward.
rule.rationale: archive_command failures are transient (network blip, S3 throttle) and often recover silently in production without any log noise. Without explicit monitoring, the failure is discovered only during a restore attempt — the worst possible moment.
